items.title: Zahid Hamidi: Election readiness primary goal for Melaka Umno meet tonight
items.summary: MELAKA, April 17 — Planning and preparations for the upcoming General Election (GE) and State Elections will be the primary focus of the Umno Melaka meeting scheduled for tonight, said party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. "This is the first of the party's meetings in the state as part of our preparations for the upcoming elections," he said during the simultaneous opening ceremony of Umno Branch Meetings for Melaka at the Melaka International Trade Centre (MITC) here today. Also present were Umno Secretary-General Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Umno Youth Chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh, and Melaka Umno Liaison Committee Chairman Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh.

items.title: Anwar orders study on restoring Langkawi’s duty-free status, to review EV policies following BYD plant concerns
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — The government will study the proposal to restore Langkawi’s duty-free status as a move to revitalise the island’s economy, which is currently facing challenges from rising costs and a decline in domestic tourism appeal. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the proposal deserves attention as Langkawi’s tourism sector has been a bit sluggish lately. Anwar also expressed his appreciation to the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, for the proposal.

items.title: inDrive introduces 1.0pct commission rate for KL drivers
items.summary: inDrive has implemented a one per cent commission rate for e-hailing drivers in Kuala Lumpur, enabling them to keep 99 per cent of their fares throughout the campaign period ending May 31. The mobility and delivery platform said the initiative is available to both new and existing drivers, providing a different earnings structure as operating costs rise. While inDrive's temporary one per cent commission is a significant outlier designed for a limited campaign period, most major competitors operate near the legal maximum or offer tiered incentives inDrive said increasing fuel prices, influenced by global market uncertainties, have added pressure to drivers' daily expenses and net income.

items.title: Ipoh City Council clears vagrants from shoplot walkways
items.summary: IPOH: The Ipoh City Council (MBI) conducted an operation on Tuesday to clear and sanitise shoplot walkways following viral videos of vagrants sleeping in the area. The operation targeted walkways around Jalan Dato Onn Jaafar and Jalan Foo Yet Kai. An MBI spokesman said the exercise involved 24 personnel, including officers from the Social Welfare Department and the National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada).

items.title: Heavy rain, thunderstorm warning in 11 states and KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for 11 states and Kuala Lumpur, with heavy rain and strong winds, until 7pm today. Areas expected to be affected are Perlis, Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu); Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak. In Kelantan, the affected areas are Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang); Terengganu (Besut and Hulu Terengganu); Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Maran, Bera and Rompin)and Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat).

items.title: No let-up for KLCC ‘photo touts’ as DBKL seizes more cameras in latest ‘Op Lens’ raid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has intensified its crackdown on unauthorised photographers, or ‘photo touts,’ around the city’s top tourist spots, seizing more equipment and issuing fresh compounds in its latest multi-agency raid on Thursday. During the Thursday raid, enforcement officers issued five compound notices to individuals found operating without a license. This latest enforcement action follows a similar raid conducted on Monday, where DBKL issued four compound notices, seized another four sets of photography equipment, and served two court appearance notices for unpaid fines.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest: Forensic analyst extracts WhatsApp chats, voice notes from seized phone
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 17 — The Coroner’s Court here has heard that WhatsApp chats and voice notes were extracted from a mobile phone tagged WF11, one of the exhibits in the case involving Zara Qairina Mahathir’s death. WF11 was among 15 mobile phones analysed by the Computer Crimes Investigation Unit. When questioned by conducting officer Sofia S Sawayan, Computer Crimes Investigation Unit analyst ASP Mohd Zaidi Abu Hassan testified that WF11 is an exhibit consisting of a mobile phone and a SIM card.

items.title: Widespread thunderstorm warning hits KL, Selangor, Penang; Friday commuters brace for heavy rain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a severe thunderstorm warning for large swathes of Peninsular Malaysia and parts of Sarawak, effective immediately until 7.00pm this evening. The warning also extends to Perlis, nearly all of Kedah, parts of Negeri Sembilan, and several districts in Johor, including Johor Bahru and Kota Tinggi. In East Malaysia, the warning covers several divisions in Sarawak, including Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, and parts of Miri and Limbang.

items.title: Indonesian envoy denies report claiming 127ha of Sebatik shifted to Indonesia, says area still a ‘grey zone’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Indonesia’s ambassador to Malaysia Datuk Raden Mohammad Iman Hascarya Kusumo has denied reports from Indonesian media alleging that part of Pulau Sebatik in Sabah is now under Indonesia’s jurisdiction. “I want to emphasise that (Pulau Sebatik) is not under Indonesia’s authority, it is still a ‘grey area’,” he said. “Reports from Indonesia are not correct.

items.title: Rohingya sea crossings hit record death toll in 2025, UNHCR says
items.summary: GENEVA: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal in 2025, making it the deadliest year on record for the route, the United Nations refugee agency said on Friday. More than one in seven of the estimated 6,500 Rohingya refugees who attempted the sea crossing last year were reported missing or dead, marking the highest mortality rate worldwide for refugee and migrant sea journeys, UNHCR spokesperson Babar Baloch told reporters in Geneva. —Reuters © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Viral bus "lap" incident woman, 8As SPM achiever, pleads to continue studies
items.summary: JASIN: The woman in a viral video showing her sitting on the lap of an express bus driver - Nur Adila Najwa Ilham Ameerrullah, 21 - told the magistrate's court here today that she hopes to continue her studies. Magistrate Mazana Sinin sentenced Arif to one year's jail and a RM5,000 fine, while Nur Adila Najwa was sentenced to six months' jail and a RM5,000 fine after both pleaded guilty when the charges were read. Nur Adila Najwa said in mitigation that she had been raised by her grandmother since infancy and later faced financial difficulties following her grandmother's death, which led her to seek employment in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: 6 injured as motorcycles collide during street racing crackdown, one catches fire
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: Six motorcycles collided while fleeing an illegal street racing crackdown, leaving one bike burnt and six riders injured on Friday. Kuala Terengganu district police chief Assistant Commissioner Azli Mohd Noor said the incident occurred during a joint 10-hour operation with the Road Transport Department (RTD), from 10pm on Thursday to 8am on Friday. "During the operation, several accidents involving six motorcycles occurred, believed to be due to riders attempting to evade inspection," he said.

items.title: Indonesia envoy rejects claims Sabah land on Sebatik now under Jakarta
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Indonesia's ambassador to Malaysia has dismissed reports that a portion of Pulau Sebatik in Sabah has been placed under Indonesian control, describing the claim as inaccurate. Datuk Raden Mohammad Iman Hascarya Kusumo said the 127.3-hectare area in question remains a "grey area", with discussions over its status still ongoing at a high level. It is still a grey area, and discussions are continuing," he said when contacted in Jakarta.

items.title: Malaysian vessel in Strait of Hormuz damaged, unable to depart
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: One of seven Malaysian fuel tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz has suffered damage and is unable to depart from a port there, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Of the vessels involved, one has already arrived in Malaysia while another is approaching national waters. "One has arrived, and another is close, entering Malaysian waters.

items.title: MPSEA calls for faster renewable energy implementation in C&I solar segment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Photovoltaic and Sustainable Energy Industry Association (MPSEA) has called on the government to urgently improve the speed and efficiency of renewable energy (RE) implementation, particularly for the commercial and industrial (C&I) solar segment. In a statement today, the non-profit organisation representing the solar and RE industry in Malaysia said Malaysia already has the policy ambition, market interest, and supporting frameworks to advance the energy transition. Without immediate improvements in implementation efficiency, Malaysia risks missing the critical window to translate strong policy into real economic, industrial, and energy security gains.

items.title: Ocean Thunder now in Malaysian waters, heading towards Pengerang
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The Ocean Thunder, a Petronas-chartered crude tanker carrying Iraqi Basrah heavy oil, has entered Malaysian waters and is now making its final approach to Pengerang, with its arrival time pushed to 7pm tonight. Vessel-tracking data from maritime monitoring systems showed the very large crude carrier (VLCC) had earlier been expected to arrive at 1.30pm after entering the Singapore Strait, but subsequent movement and operational coordination requirements have shifted its estimated berthing window. The 251-degree course indicates the Ocean Thunder is on a west-southwest inbound track through the Singapore Strait, aligning itself with Malaysian waters for the final pilotage and berthing approach into Pengerang.

items.title: MICCI flags rising freight, insurance costs as Strait of Hormuz tensions strain supply chains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian International Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MICCI) has warned of rising freight rates and insurance premiums following disruptions linked to a US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with cost pressures already filtering through global supply chains, though impacts vary across industries. "Amid evolving conditions, we underscore the importance of clear, consistent and timely communication from the government to industry stakeholders to enable better planning and coordination," she said. Any disruption could impact global shipping flows, including vessel availability, freight rates and overall supply chain reliability.

items.title: Man, 37, faces murder charge in death of nine-month-old baby in Klang
items.summary: KLANG, April 17 — A factory supervisor was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a nine-month-old boy earlier this month. Mohd Syahril Nazrul Ruslim, 37, is accused of causing the death of the infant at an apartment unit in Taman Bayu Perdana here at about 3.30 pm on April 6. No plea was recorded from the accused after the charge was read before Magistrate A. Khartiyayini, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.

items.title: Factory supervisor charged with murder of nine-month-old boy
items.summary: KLANG: A factory supervisor was charged at the magistrate's court here with the murder of a nine-month-old boy earlier this month. Mohd Syahril Nazrul Ruslim, 37, is accused of causing the infant's death at an apartment unit in Taman Bayu Perdana around 3.30pm on April 6. No plea was recorded after the charge was read before magistrate A. Khartiyayini, as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.

items.title: Shared responsibility: Anwar says students not exempt from cleaning toilets
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended the practice of students cleaning school toilets, describing it as a necessary lesson in responsibility and discipline. He said the move should not be disputed as it applies to all students, regardless of their family background or social standing. He said cleaning toilets should not be viewed as the sole responsibility of cleaners.

items.title: MCBA to be equipped with firearms by year-end, says Home Minister
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) is set to be equipped with firearms by the end of the year. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said several options are currently being studied, including the use of taser guns and light firearms such as pistols. "The bottom line is that we agree MCBA must be equipped with the necessary weapons," he said during a press conference at the Al-Quran Veneration programme aboard Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency's KM Tun Fatimah, here.

items.title: China expected to keep benchmark lending rates steady after strong GDP data
items.summary: SHANGHAI: China is expected to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for an 11th consecutive month in April, a Reuters survey showed, as robust first-quarter growth and a pick-up in inflation have weakened the case for additional monetary stimulus. This week, China's economy logged 5.0 per cent growth, picking up from 4.5 per cent in the previous quarter, and at the top of its full-year target range. They now expect China to keep official interest rates steady this year.

items.title: [UPDATED] Rafizi's ex-aide James Chai to return on April 28 to assist MACC probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Political analyst James Chai is expected to return to Malaysia on April 28 to assist the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in its investigation into a RM1.11 billion government-backed semiconductor project. MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki confirmed to New Straits Times that Chai's return had been communicated to the commission, adding that investigators had briefed him on the matter. The firm said Chai would be available to assist investigators on April 28 at 10am, and that the MACC had confirmed the timing.

items.title: Agriculture Ministry welcomes Selangor’s RM131m plan to boost food sector resilience
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM) has welcomed the Selangor government’s initiative to introduce the Selangor Resilience Enhancement Package, which involves an allocation of RM131 million, to address current challenges, especially the impact of the global energy crisis on production costs in the agro-food sector. Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said that the Selangor state government’s commitment reflected the collective approach needed in the current situation. Mohamad said that the move was in line with and complemented various initiatives by the federal government through KPKM in assisting target groups nationwide.

items.title: Ford recalls about 1.4 million pickup trucks over gearshift issue
items.summary: NEW YORK: Ford is recalling about 1.4 million F-150 pickup trucks in the US following a US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) investigation into reports of unexpected downshifts, the regulator said on Friday. The NHTSA said Ford was aware of two "potentially" related injuries and one accident, and that dealers would update the trucks' powertrain control module software as a remedy. Earlier this year, the regulator had expanded a safety-related investigation into the issue.

items.title: Amazon Web Services to expand presence in Malaysia as Anwar urges more local companies to join in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia’s infrastructure strength and supply chain efficiency are among the key factors behind the success of Amazon Web Services’ (AWS) investments in the country, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post today, he emphasised that AWS should continue expanding opportunities for local companies to participate more meaningfully in its global ecosystem. “The government will continue strengthening existing policies to ensure long-term benefits from this strategic investment, while also encouraging broader collaboration between local suppliers and their data centre and cloud technology ecosystem,” he said.

items.title: UK's Workspace warns of 'substantial' profit drop for fiscal 2027, shares fall
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Flexible office-space provider Workspace on Friday warned of a "substantial step down" in fiscal 2027 profits from 2026 amid falling rents and higher costs, sending its shares down nearly 15 per cent. Total rent roll fell 1.4 per cent to 127.3 million pounds (US$171.93 million) in the three months to March 31, while stabilised portfolio rent per square foot dipped 0.9 per cent to 46.31 pounds. The company's shares were down 14.1 per cent at 320.6 pence in early trading.

items.title: Khaled: Lebanon-Israel ceasefire a step forward, but risks remain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel marks a positive step towards easing tensions, but the situation on the ground remains uncertain, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He noted reports of several ceasefire violations in southern Lebanon, underscoring the fluid security environment. "The Defence Ministry's priority remains focused on the safety of Malaysian Battalion (Malbatt) 850-13 personnel, who are carrying out their responsibilities under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (Unifil) mission," he said in a statement.

items.title: French train maker Alstom hit by delivery delays
items.summary: PARIS: Alstom shares plummeted Friday after the French train maker warned that "execution headwinds" had delayed deliveries to clients, forcing the group to abandon its profit and cash flow targets. Free cash flow dropped to around 330 million euros for its full year, compared with 502 million the year before, and Alstom said it would not meet its goal of a total cash flow of 1.5 billion euros in the three years to March 2027. It also said profit margins would not improve to 8-10 per cent next year as promised.

items.title: Sabah CM denies Sebatik land transfer claim amid conflicting reports from Indonesia
items.summary: LAHAD DATU, April 17 — Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor has denied that 127.3 hectares on Pulau Sebatik, previously within Malaysian territory, now belongs to Indonesia. “Who said it? Indonesian media?

items.title: Nestlé Malaysia hits 100pct local chilli sourcing milestone for Maggi sauce
items.summary: The achievement underscores the company's long-term commitment to strengthening national food security while uplifting local farming communities. The milestone was highlighted during an official visit by Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir to the People's Income Initiative-Agropreneur (IPR-INTAN) Putrajaya chilli farming project. The project, which operates under the Nestlé Chilli Club since 1995, supports farmers across 12 sites in Kelantan, Selangor, Putrajaya and Johor, spanning across 146 acres.

items.title: Real estate transaction scams: What buyers need to know
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Buying or selling property is one of the largest financial transactions in a person's life. Impersonating real estate agents Fraud by individuals posing as real estate agents is becoming more common and often targets victims who are looking for affordable housing. There are also cases of forgery of real estate documents.

items.title: British PM under renewed fire over ex-envoy to US appointment
items.summary: LONDON: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faced mounting pressure on Friday over his appointment of Peter Mandelson as envoy to Washington, after it emerged the veteran politician had failed security vetting. The government said foreign ministry officials were responsible for the latest failure, adding that neither Starmer nor his foreign minister were informed of the failed security check until this week. Foreign ministry officials had allowed Mandelson's appointment to proceed "against the recommendation of UK Security Vetting", the government said.

items.title: Malaysia’s first womb surgery for spine condition sees baby doing well after birth
items.summary: KUCHING, April 17 — A baby who underwent in-utero fetal surgery to repair spina bifida at Sarawak General Hospital (SGH) here in September last year is now in good condition. Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad said the four-month-old infant is growing well, with clinical assessments showing the baby is able to breathe, swallow and pass urine and stool normally. VP shunt is a procedure to drain excess fluid from the brain to the abdomen, helping to relieve pressure and prevent damage caused by fluid build-up.

items.title: ‘Let’s see’: Anwar doesn’t rule out minister pay cuts if energy crisis escalates
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he is not discounting suggestions that ministers take a pay cut if the global energy crisis worsens and raises pressure on government spending. Still, Anwar told reporters here he would prefer the discussion focus on Cabinet members’ performance instead, arguing that their salaries are already comparatively lower. “Relatively, their salaries are already lower, so the more important thing now is that they perform,” he said in response to a question from the press.

items.title: Lebanese trickle home as ceasefire with Israel mostly holds
items.summary: BEIRUT: People displaced by the war in Lebanon began returning home on Friday to check on their properties, though some remained cautious, saying a fragile ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel may not hold. But he said conditions remained difficult. Hezbollah attacks killed two Israeli civilians and 13 Israeli soldiers since March 2, Israel said.

items.title: Heartbreak to handbreak: love spat turns violent
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A dispute believed to have stemmed from a love triangle turned violent at Karpal Singh Drive, police said. Northeast district deputy police chief Lee Swee Sake said a report was lodged on April 10, with seven individuals arrested to assist in investigations. Lee added that investigation papers have been referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor, and police are awaiting further instructions.

items.title: Terengganu orders action as DOE identifies vessel behind 3km-long Pantai Teluk Kalong oil spill
items.summary: CHUKAI, April 17 — The Terengganu state government has directed its Department of Environment (DOE) to take strict action against the ship identified as the source of the oil spill pollution at Pantai Teluk Kalong here since last Monday. If necessary, we will seize the involved ship,” he said when met by reporters after inspecting the oil spill pollution at Pantai Teluk Kalong here today. In addition, Razali said the Kemaman Municipal Council (MPK) has also begun cleaning up the oil residues on the beach since yesterday.

items.title: Govt may absorb affected overseas students into local varsities if Middle East conflict worsens
items.summary: JASIN, April 17 — The government is open and ready to place Malaysian students who are stranded or whose studies are affected in countries involved in the West Asia crisis into local higher education institutions (IPT) if the situation requires. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said this is among the Ministry of Higher Education’s (MOHE) preparedness measures and guarantees to ensure that the studies of affected students are not disrupted, and this includes those who have just received offers to continue their studies in the affected countries. “So far, there are no official figures on the students affected (due to the crisis in West Asia) because universities in the affected countries are still functioning as usual and the number, except for Jordan and Egypt, is relatively small.

items.title: Apple's iPhone shipments in China surge 20pct in Q1, data shows
items.summary: BEIJING: Apple's iPhone shipments surged 20 per cent in China in the first quarter, for the strongest growth among major vendors, despite an overall decline as rising prices of memory chips boosted costs, data from Counterpoint Research showed. Overall smartphone shipments dropped 4.0 per cent in the world's largest smartphone market in the period from January to March, hit by supply chain disruptions and the soaring chip prices. But China's two largest smartphone vendors, telecoms giant Huawei and Apple, bucked the trend, reporting growth of 2.0 per cent and 20 per cent respectively.

items.title: Anwar says proposal for students to clean school toilets aims to teach responsibility, humility
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today defended a suggestion he made earlier this month that cleaning school toilets be made a fixed curriculum, saying it is meant to teach youths civic responsibility. Speaking to reporters after performing Friday prayers here, Anwar called on parents to support the initiative as a way to help nurture a sense of humility in children. “I’d like to advise our youths, none is exempted and none come from privileged backgrounds.

items.title: Woman convicted of murdering brother with boyfriend over phone dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A woman has been convicted by the High Court of murdering her elder brother with her boyfriend in Taiping over a mobile phone dispute seven years ago. Evidence indicated that the victim had been incapacitated before being restrained with his hands tied behind his back during the attack on July 5, 2019. The victim's decomposing body was discovered two days later behind a neighbouring unoccupied house, wrapped in a grey comforter and a plastic bag, with his hands still tied behind his back.

items.title: MOHE ready to place students affected by Middle East crisis in local universities
items.summary: JASIN: The government is prepared to place Malaysian students affected by the ongoing crisis in the Middle East into local higher education institutions if the need arises. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the move is part of the ministry's contingency plan to ensure students can continue their studies without disruption, including those who have recently received offers to study in affected countries. He said discussions would typically focus on credit transfers based on guidelines set by the Malaysian Qualifications Agency to avoid students having to repeat their studies.

items.title: Malaysia to join Macron, Starmer meeting on Hormuz Strait energy crisis today, says Anwar
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — Malaysia will be attending a high-level meeting organised by France and the UK to discuss the energy crisis stemming from a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim confirmed today. Anwar told reporters here that he spoke on the phone with French president Emmanuel Macron, who extended the invitation personally, adding that Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan will represent Malaysia. Malaysia not selling fuel to Australia Meanwhile, Anwar dismissed claims that Putrajaya is selling fuel to Australia, an allegation that surfaced following a meeting with his counterpart Anthony Albanese, yesterday.

items.title: [UPDATED] UM refutes claims of 'cancer cluster' caused by hospital SOP breaches
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya (UM) has refuted claims linking radiation from a PET-CT machine at the UM Medical Centre to cancer cases among several staff members. UM said PET-CT scan operations are conducted according to strict safety standards, and radiation exposure levels are controlled within established limits. UM said it complies with the Radiation Protection Program Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), the Atomic Energy Licensing Act, and relevant 2010 and 2011 radiation protection and waste management regulations.

items.title: Anwar: One tanker in Malaysian waters while Hormuz tension delays five others, Putrajaya monitoring
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — Five Malaysia-bound oil tankers remain in the Strait of Hormuz amid a new US-imposed blockade that is disrupting global oil flows, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Last week, Reuters reported that Petco, a unit of Malaysian state energy firm Petronas, was among seven Malaysia-linked vessels cleared by Iran to transit the strait, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters. Anwar had said in March that Tehran would allow Malaysian vessels to pass through ‌the ⁠strait after holding talks with Iranian officials.

items.title: Ex-prisoner jailed after exposing himself to woman in Klang
items.summary: KLANG: An unemployed man has been jailed for two months after pleading guilty to exposing himself to a woman. Magistrate A. Karthiyayini sentenced Muhammad Rakis Abdul Lama, 38, after he admitted the charge on Friday. According to the charge, the man, who had recently been released from prison, insulted the modesty of a 21-year-old woman by exposing himself at a house in Teluk Pulai, Klang, on April 12.

items.title: Rafizi’s ex-aide James Chai to return, meet MACC on April 28 in RM1.11b semiconductor probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Political analyst James Chai will return to Malaysia later this month to assist the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in its ongoing investigation linked to a RM1.11 billion government-backed semiconductor project involving UK-based ARM Holdings. Local law firm Kesavan Advocates and Solicitors, which said it is acting for Chai, confirmed that arrangements have been made for his return following approval from his employer in London. Chai was a former special functions officer to ex-economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.

items.title: Transport Ministry rolls out tiered ferry surcharge from April 20, exemptions for daily commuters and locals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The government will introduce a targeted bunker fuel surcharge on passenger and roll-on roll-off ferry services from April 20 to offset rising marine diesel costs. The surcharge rates are as follows: Up to RM4.00/litre: 0 per cent RM4.01 to RM5.00/litre: 5 per cent RM5.01 to RM6.00/litre: 8 per cent RM6.01 to RM7.00/litre: 12 per cent RM7.01 to RM8.00/litre: 15 per cent Above RM8.00/litre: 18 per cent (maximum) The ministry said the monthly review mechanism will allow adjustments based on market conditions and subsidy positions to ensure fares remain manageable. “The bunker fuel surcharge will take effect from 20 April 2026 until further notice, subject to Government decision,” the ministry said.

items.title: Police looking through CCTV to trace suspects in RM9.7m Brickfields jewellery heist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Police are analysing closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage from multiple locations to trace suspects who fled with RM9.7 million worth of jewellery in a Brickfields robbery yesterday. Brickfields police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said recordings from the shop and surrounding areas are being examined to map the suspects’ escape route, Malay daily Kosmo! Four men wearing helmets and armed with two pistols robbed a jewellery shop along Jalan Tun Sambanthan at about 11am yesterday.

items.title: Police caution public over unlawful social media use linked to seditious, provocative debates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Police have urged the public to avoid turning social media into a platform for unlawful and provocative debates that could disrupt public order. “In recent times, social media has increasingly been used as a platform to spread posts or comments containing seditious elements and provocative debates on government policies and current issues, leading to controversy among users.” “This trend not only disrupts harmony in cyberspace but also creates an unhealthy digital ecosystem that could spark tension within society if not curbed through enforcement,” it said. CID director Datuk M. Kumar said such actions contravene existing laws, particularly the Sedition Act 1948, which prohibits acts, speech or publications that incite hatred against the monarchy or the government.

items.title: Digital push pays off as Penang advances towards fully cashless state by 2030
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17— Cashless transactions in Penang have recorded strong growth, with the adoption rate reaching 97.92 per cent last year, placing the state among the highest in digital payment usage nationwide. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said the achievement reflects the success of the state government’s digitalisation initiatives, particularly in expanding cashless services across local authorities. “The 97.92 per cent cashless transaction rate clearly reflects the high level of public confidence and acceptance of digital payment systems.

items.title: Zara Qairina inquest: Lawyer plans to recall psychiatrist, bring in up to 11 expert witnesses
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 17 — The Coroner’s Court was told today that the lawyer representing the mother of 13-year-old Zara Qairina Mahathir plans to call several expert witnesses in the ongoing inquest into her death. Counsel Datuk Rizwandean M. Borhan said six expert witnesses have been identified, including handwriting and document examiners Dr Linthini Gannetion, Dr Tay Eue Kam and Dr Dzulkiflee Ismail, as well as forensic science expert Dr Wan Nur Syuhaila Mat Desa. He added that child psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli and psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo will also be called to testify, alongside several other witnesses, including the victim’s family members.

items.title: Bus driver, girlfriend get one‑year and six‑month jail terms in viral ‘lap‑driving’ case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — An express bus driver and his girlfriend were sentenced to jail today after pleading guilty in the Magistrates’ Court to dangerous driving last week. Sinar Harian reported that Arif Fahmi Abd Salam, 36, and Nur Adila Najwa Ilham Ameerrullah, 21, entered their pleas before Magistrate Mazana Sinin. Arif Fahmi received a one‑year jail term, while Nur Adila Najwa was sentenced to six months’ imprisonment, with both also fined RM5,000.

items.title: JPJ probes claims of vehicle number plates being transferred without owners’ knowledge
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has confirmed receiving several complaints regarding alleged transfers of ownership of unique vehicle registration numbers without the original owners’ knowledge. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the department will conduct detailed investigations before taking any action. He said JPJ will carry out investigations and take appropriate action based on existing procedures and laws.

items.title: JPJ to review viral video of express bus driver allegedly driving dangerously near KM196.1 southbound
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) will review a recent viral video allegedly showing a bus driver driving dangerously. JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said they will need to verify the case first. He said cases involving criminal elements or accidents will first be referred to the police before any further action is taken.

items.title: JPJ to launch ‘P‑P’ special plate series for 80th anniversary, bidding opens April 20 from RM300
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 17 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) today announced the launch of a special “P-P” vehicle registration series in conjunction with its 80th anniversary. Bidding for the special “P-P” series is set to open from April 20 to April 24, according to JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Minimum bid prices remain consistent with existing categories, starting from RM300 for running numbers, RM500 for popular numbers, RM2,500 for attractive numbers, RM5,000 for premium numbers, and RM20,000 for golden numbers.

items.title: No room for complacency despite upgraded World Bank economic growth forecast, says Mida chairman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — Malaysia is not only focusing on economic growth but also emphasising building a more resilient and diversified economy in the face of global uncertainties, said Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. This follows the World Bank last week raising Malaysia’s growth forecast to 4.4 per cent from 4.1 per cent for 2026, despite increasing global uncertainties, including the West Asia conflict. Tengku Zafrul, who is the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman, said the development showed that the country’s economy continued to grow but warned that Malaysia should not get too complacent.

items.title: Govt farming scheme earns RM22m, helps over 2,300 Malaysians earn extra income
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The People’s Income Initiative-Agricultural Entrepreneurship (IPR-Intan) programme under the Ministry of Economy has generated cumulative revenue of about RM22 million since its introduction in 2023, said Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. “One of the (IPR programme) segments is Intan, which focuses on agriculture, and so far it has produced about 2,300 participants with cumulative revenue reaching RM22 million,” he told reporters after visiting the IPR-Intan project site here today. Meanwhile, in Putrajaya, Akmal Nasrullah said the chilli cultivation project using fertigation on 4.04 hectares has benefited 20 participants, each earning an average monthly income of about RM3,000.

items.title: Malaysia’s inflation remains modest but ticks up in March 2026, govt data shows
items.summary: According to DOSM, the insurance and financial services group recorded 4.9 per cent (February 2026: 4.7 per cent); alcoholic beverages and tobacco, 2.7 per cent (February 2026: 2.6 per cent); restaurant and accommodation services, 2.6 per cent (February 2026: 2.5 per cent); health, 1.4 per cent (February 2026: 1.2 per cent); information and communication, 1.4 per cent (February 2026: 0.5 per cent); housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels, 1.2 per cent (February 2026: 1.1 per cent); and recreation, sport and culture, 1.0 per cent, (February 2026: 0.8 per cent). This was driven by subgroups of food away from home, 2.3 per cent (February 2026: 2.4 per cent), and food at home, 0.1 per cent (February 2026: 0.3 per cent). This was driven by the transport group (2.1 per cent, February 2026: 0.2 per cent); information and communication, (0.8 per cent, February 2026: -0.2 per cent); insurance and financial services (0.3 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent); health (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent); recreation, sport and culture (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent) and education (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.1 per cent).
